title: cerinthe gymnandra subsp. oranensis
intro: Cerinthe gymnandra subsp. oranensis, also known as Rock-seed or Honeywort, is a perennial plant in the borage family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cerinthe gymnandra subsp. oranensis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to treat various skin conditions and wounds.
